---
title: "PA 5.2:  Jewel Heist"
subtitle: "Dates and Times with `lubridate`"
format: html
  embed-resources: true
editor: source
execute: 
  error: true
  echo: true
  message: false
  warning: false
---

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
```

## Solve the Mystery

Just down the road in Montecito, CA several rare jewels went missing. The jewels were stolen and replaced with fakes, but detectives have not been able to solve the case. They are now calling in a data scientist to help parse their clues.

A camera was located near the building where the jewels went missing, so the detectives have provided you with a list of people who may have entered the building. This list includes the date and time they were spotted on the camera, **in Pacific Time (PT)**.

Unfortunately, the date and time of the jewel heist is not known. You have been hired to crack the case. Use the clues below to discover the thief's identity.

```{r}
# 214 total suspects
suspects <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zoerehnberg/STAT331-S23/main/practice_activities/suspects.csv")
```

::: callout-note

**Time Zones** will be very important to find the right suspect!

:::

0. When data is read into `R`, dates are automatically read in as the UTC time zone. The first thing that we need to do is tell `R` that the times listed are actually in **PT**. Fill in the code below to do this.

```{r}
suspects <- suspects |> 
  mutate(Time.Spotted = force_tz(Time.Spotted, tzone = "America/Los_Angeles"))
```


1. Based on the cleaning schedule for the room where the jewels are held, the heist was not committed in the morning  (i.e. it was committed at 12:00pm PCT or later).

```{r}
# end with 112 suspects left

suspects <- suspects |> 
  filter(hour(Time.Spotted) >= 12)
```

2. The room where the heist was committed is closed on Tuesdays and Thursdays (and there were no signs of forced entry), so the heist did **not** happen on those days. *Hint: if you are getting something weird, it is because you did not set the time zone correctly in step 0!!*.

```{r}
# end with 78 suspects left
suspects <- suspects |> 
  filter(wday(Time.Spotted) != 3, wday(Time.Spotted) != 5)
```


3. It is believed that the heist was committed within 35 days of Thanksgiving 2022 (before or after).

::: callout-tip
# Hints
Pay attention to **time zones**! 

Thanksgiving 2022 was on November 24th.

I would recommend using an interval and `%within%`
:::

```{r}
# end with 11 suspects left
thanksgiving <- ymd("2022-11-24", tz = "America/Los_Angeles")

suspects <- suspects |> 
  filter(
    Time.Spotted %within% interval(
      thanksgiving - days(35),
      thanksgiving + days(35)
    )
  )
```

4. The detectives partially decoded a message from the thief to a notorious fence in Iceland. In it, the thief said the job would be done "after the sun sets for you, but before midnight." 

::: callout-tip
# Hints
In November, the sun sets at 4:00pm in Iceland.

Pay attention to time zones! Iceland is actually in the "UTC" timezone.
:::

```{r}
# end with 4
suspects <- suspects |> 
  filter(
    hour(with_tz(Time.Spotted, tzone = "UTC")) > 16,
    hour(with_tz(Time.Spotted, tzone = "UTC")) < 24
  )
```

5. The thief left behind a receipt at the scene of the crime. The receipt is smudged, but the day of the month is shown to be 22. It is thought that the heist took place no more than three days **after** the receipt was issued.

```{r}
suspects <- suspects |> 
  filter(mday(Time.Spotted) >= 22,
         mday(Time.Spotted) <= 25)
```

6. The thief is amused by your efforts and has sent you a cryptic clue:

> "The exact number of seconds between midnight on Jan 1, 1970 and the time I arrived on the scene is divisible by 6."

:::callout-tip
# Hint

Check out how date-time objects are stored on the `lubridate` cheatsheet.
:::

```{r}
suspects <- suspects |> 
  filter(
    second(Time.Spotted) %% 6 == 0
  )
```

::: callout-note

### Canvas Quiz Submission

Who is the thief? *Only one name should remain.* Remember that you can check with classmates and me about the answer!

:::
